cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
come: verb. To move nearer to the point of perspective.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
mem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mangala.
memo: noun. A short note; a memorandum.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mome: noun. A dull or stupid person.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
neem: noun. Azadirachta indica, a large, mostly evergreen tree from India, whose seeds yield neem oil, the insecticide azadirachtin.
nom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nocamán.
nome: noun. A prefecture or unit of regional government in Greece.
omen: noun. Something which portends or is perceived to portend either a good or evil event or circumstance in the future, or which causes a
once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
